heading: Purpose
label: 4102 Purpose
text: The Congress declares as its intent and purpose that there shall be an effective (1) job and job training intensive services program, (2) employment placement service program, and (3) job training placement service program for eligible veterans and eligible persons and that, to this end policies and regulations shall be promulgated and administered by an Assistant Secretary of Labor for Veterans’ Employment and Training, established by , through a Veterans’ Employment and Training Service within the Department of Labor, so as to provide such veterans and persons the maximum of employment and training opportunities, with priority given to the needs of disabled veterans and veterans who served on active duty during a war or in a campaign or expedition for which a campaign badge has been authorized through existing programs, coordination and merger of programs and implementation of new programs, including programs carried out by the Veterans’ Employment and Training Service to implement all efforts to ease the transition of servicemembers to civilian careers that are consistent with, or an outgrowth of, the military experience of the servicemembers.section 4102A of this title
notes: Prior ProvisionsPrior section 4102, , , ; , , ; , , , related to divisions of Department of Medicine and Surgery, prior to repeal by , , . See .Pub. L. 85–85772 Stat. 1243Pub. Amendments2002—, substituted “job and job training intensive services program,” for “job and job training counseling service program,”.Pub. L. 107–288, § 5(a)(1)(B), substituted “and veterans who served on active duty during a war or in a campaign or expedition for which a campaign badge has been authorized” for “and veterans of the Vietnam era” and inserted before period “, including programs carried out by the Veterans’ Employment and Training Service to implement all efforts to ease the transition of servicemembers to civilian careers that are consistent with, or an outgrowth of, the military experience of the servicemembers”.Pub. L. 107–288, § 5(b)(1)1991— renumbered as this section and substituted “4102A” for “2002A”.Pub. L. 102–83section 2002 of this title1988— inserted “and Training” after “for Veterans’ Employment” and substituted “Veterans’ Employment and Training Service” for “Veterans Employment Service”.Pub. L. 100–3231983— substituted “an Assistant” for “a Assistant”.Pub. L. 98–1601982— inserted “and regulations” after “to this end policies”, and inserted “, with priority given to the needs of disabled veterans and veterans of the Vietnam era” after “opportunities”.Pub. L. 97–3061980— struck out “Deputy” before “Assistant Secretary”.Pub. L. 96–4661976— inserted “by a Deputy Assistant Secretary of Labor for Veteran’s Employment, established by ,” after “promulgated and administered”.Pub. L. 94–502section 2002A of this title1974— substituted “eligible veterans and eligible persons” for “eligible veterans” and “to provide such veterans and persons” for “to provide such veterans”.Pub. L. 93–5081972— substituted provisions stating the Congressional declaration of purpose, for provisions relating to the assignment of veterans’ employment representatives. See .Pub. L. 92–540section 2003 of this title1966— inserted “or of service after ” after “veterans of any war” in cls. (3) and (5); “or of service after ” after “veteran of any war” in first sentence; and “or of service after ,” after “veterans of any war” in cls. (1) and (4), wherever appearing, respectively.Pub.
